turns.user_message: I tried to just go for a long walk and clear my head like I always do, but the heavy feeling just followed me right back inside. It is so frustrating that something as simple as stepping outside used to fix my mood in minutes. Now I just sit there staring at the wall, completely drained by my own thoughts. I keep telling myself to just shake it off, but the guilt just piles up instead. I feel so stupid for letting a bad afternoon ruin my entire evening.

I keep trying to distract myself with random tasks, hoping the constant motion will drown out this nagging voice in my head. It used to be so easy to just brush off a rough patch and move forward without a second thought. Lately though, every little setback sticks to me like glue and I end up feeling deeply ashamed of my own reactions. I am exhausted from pretending everything is fine when I am clearly failing to handle my own emotions. The more I try to push it away, the heavier the disappointment gets.

I threw myself into an intense organizing session today, convinced that sorting through everything would finally wash away this awful weight. Back in the day, a simple reset like that would instantly snap me back to my usual self. Now I just stand in the middle of a perfectly arranged space feeling completely hollow and utterly disgusted with my own inability to just be normal. I am so tired of watching myself fall apart over things that should not matter this much. It feels like I am fundamentally broken, and no amount of busywork is ever going to fix the shame I carry.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with a failed attempt to feel better. This used to not bother the speaker.
